displayName: IELTS — International English Language Testing System
shortDescription: British Council / IDP · Academic & General Training · Band 0–9 · 2-year validity

policyAlert.summary: Verify IELTS exam fee and test dates on britishcouncil.in or ieltsidpindia.com before quoting. Academic vs General Training must match client goal. UK visa routes may require IELTS UKVI — confirm before booking. Never guarantee a band score.

alert.title: Academic vs General Training
alert.body: University and most study visas require IELTS Academic. Canada PR and UK settlement typically require General Training (or Academic where stated). Confirm module before enrollment and exam registration.

about.label: Description
about.value: Standardized English proficiency test measuring Listening, Reading, Writing, and Speaking. Jointly owned by British Council, IDP, and Cambridge. Used for study, work, and migration worldwide.

about.label: Modules
about.value: Listening (30 min) · Reading (60 min) · Writing (60 min) · Speaking (11–14 min). L/R/W on same day; Speaking may be same day or within 7 days before/after.

about.label: Academic vs GT
about.value: Academic: university & most study visas. General Training: Canada PR/citizenship, UK settlement, work migration. Same Listening & Speaking; different Reading & Writing.

about.label: Test authority
about.value: British Council & IDP — register at britishcouncil.in/exam/ielts or ieltsidpindia.com. FLC prepares students; exam fee is never included in coaching fee.

about.label: Delivery modes
about.value: Paper-based, Computer-delivered (CD-IELTS), IELTS UKVI (SELT for UK visas), One Skill Retake (OSR) where available.

about.label: India test centers
about.value: Ahmedabad, Bengaluru, Chandigarh, Chennai, Delhi, Hyderabad, Kolkata, Mumbai, Pune, and other cities — verify current list on booking portal.

about.label: After the test
about.value: TRF (Test Report Form) issued; up to 5 free electronic sends to institutions. Hand TRF to admissions or visa team when target met.

rows.label: USA — F-1 admission
values: TOEFL more common; many accept IELTS

eligibility.criterion: Valid passport for registration and test day
Name on registration matches passport exactly
Test date allows TRF before visa / uni deadline
Minimum age — no upper limit; minors need guardian consent per center

dos: Bring the same valid passport used at registration
Confirm Academic vs GT and UKVI on confirmation email
Check headphones during Listening before sections start
Manage time in Writing — Task 2 carries more weight than Task 1
Speak naturally in Speaking — extend answers in Parts 2 and 3
Report audio or technical problems to invigilator immediately
Keep confirmation email and center address accessible
donts: Do not bring phones, smartwatches, or bags into the test room
Do not bring passport photocopies — original only
Do not talk to other candidates during the test
Do not leave your seat without permission
Do not use memorized template essays word-for-word
Do not argue with the Speaking examiner about your score
Do not book Speaking on a conflicting day without checking schedule
Do not use correction fluid on paper tests where not permitted
checklist: Correct test center and reporting time
Counselor notified if first-time test taker (optional FLC briefing)

redFlagsBanner: Wrong module, name mismatch, or mocks far below target — pause exam booking and fix before test day. Never guarantee a band score.

redFlags.title: GT student booked Academic (or reverse)
redFlags.description: Module does not match migration or study pathway.
redFlags.fix: Cancel and rebook correct module before test day

redFlags.title: UK visa but standard IELTS booked
redFlags.description: UKVI SELT required for many UK visa routes.
redFlags.fix: Rebook IELTS UKVI at approved center

redFlags.title: Name ≠ passport
redFlags.fix: Contact BC/IDP to correct before test day

redFlags.title: Mocks far below target
redFlags.fix: Postpone exam; extra mocks and skill work

redFlags.title: Expired passport at booking
redFlags.description: Cannot test or TRF may be invalid.

faqs.q: Academic vs General Training — which one?
faqs.a: Academic for university and most study visas. GT for Canada PR, citizenship, and many work/settlement routes. Confirm with destination country rules.

faqs.q: Who pays the IELTS exam fee?
faqs.a: The student pays British Council or IDP directly when registering. FLC coaching fee is separate.
Same content and scoring. CD-IELTS usually has faster results (3–5 days). Speaking is still face-to-face with examiner.
No. We set targets from diagnostics and mocks. Outcomes depend on student effort, attendance, and exam performance.

faqs.q: Is TOEFL accepted for Canada PR?
faqs.a: No. IRCC accepts IELTS (GT or Academic per program) and CELPIP-General — not TOEFL or Duolingo for immigration.
Secure English Language Test format required for many UK visa applications. Book UKVI at an approved SELT center — standard IELTS may not count.
Two years from the test date for most immigration and university purposes.
Retake one section within 60 days of a full test where offered. Confirm eligibility on ielts.org before advising.
Up to five institutions included; additional sends may incur a fee.

faqs.q: Speaking on a different day — is that normal?
faqs.a: Yes. Speaking may be scheduled up to 7 days before or after L/R/W.

compliance: Never guarantee an IELTS band score
Separate coaching fee from exam registration fee on all quotes
Verify exam fee on official BC/IDP portal before quoting
Confirm Academic vs GT and UKVI before enrollment and booking
Record diagnostic, mock scores, and TRF on client file
proTips: Use CLB mapping for every Canada lead — counselors must know 6.0 = CLB 7
Book exam only after two mocks within 0.5 band of target
For UK routes, check UKVI requirement before any booking
Brief first-time test takers using the Test day guide tab
Link TRF to visa/admissions file as soon as results arrive
postApproval: Upload scores to client CRM record
Notify admissions or visa team with breakdown L/R/W/S
Plan retake, OSR, or booster if below target

timeline.title: Coaching, homework, skill drills, speaking mocks
Exam registration, test day briefing, TRF follow-up

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Initial IELTS test reference — acceptance matrix, test day guide, module samples.

staffNotes.author: Coaching team
staffNotes.text: This is the shared IELTS test reference. Program-specific batches (Regular, Crash, GT) link here for official test facts.

dos: Confirm Academic vs GT for every lead
Brief students with test day do's before exam
Keep coaching and exam fees separate on quotes
Track mocks weekly — not just overall average
donts: Quote exam fee without verifying on BC/IDP
Book exam before mocks support target
Send GT student to Academic exam without confirmation
Skip UKVI check for UK visa clients
mistakes: Wrong name on registration vs passport
Standard IELTS for UK Student Route when UKVI required
Visa team not updated when TRF arrives

quiz.question: IELTS Academic is typically required for:
quiz.explanation: Universities and most study pathways require Academic.

quiz.question: IELTS score validity is generally:
quiz.explanation: Two years from test date for most uses.

quiz.question: For Canada Express Entry FSW, CLB 7 equals approximately:
quiz.explanation: CLB 7 maps to IELTS 6.0 in each skill.

quiz.question: TOEFL iBT is accepted by IRCC for Canadian PR:
quiz.explanation: IRCC does not accept TOEFL for immigration.

quiz.question: On test day, the student must bring:
Which item is prohibited in the IELTS test room?
quiz.explanation: Phones and smartwatches must be stored outside.

quiz.question: UK Student Route often requires:
quiz.explanation: Many UK visas require UKVI SELT format.

quiz.question: IELTS exam fee is paid to:
quiz.explanation: Exam fee is separate from coaching.

quiz.question: General Training is commonly used for:
quiz.explanation: GT is standard for many migration pathways.

quiz.question: The correct order on test day for L/R/W is:
One Skill Retake (OSR) allows:
quiz.explanation: OSR retakes one section where offered.

quiz.question: Before booking the exam, FLC policy recommends:
